name: P&L Dashboard
description: Profit & loss analytics dashboard with gross income, expenses, NOI, cash flow, breakdowns by category, property comparison, and projections.

standard.description: Full profit-and-loss dashboard with income and expense breakdowns, property comparison, and projections

slots.summary-cards: Gross income, expenses, NOI, cash flow
slots.income-breakdown: Rental income, other income by category

visual_brief: Financial dashboard with a header containing title, property/portfolio selector, and date range filter. A summary metrics row shows gross income, total expenses, NOI (net operating income), and cash flow as large value cards with trend indicators. Below, two side-by-side breakdown panels: income categories (rent, fees, other) and expense categories (maintenance, insurance, utilities, management) displayed as horizontal bar charts or itemized lists with amounts. A property comparison table shows these metrics per property. A projections section shows a line chart of projected vs. actual income/expenses.

responsive.mobile: Summary metrics in a 2x2 grid. Breakdown panels stack vertically. Property comparison becomes a scrollable card list. Projections chart takes full width with simplified labels.
responsive.tablet: Metrics in a single row. Breakdowns side by side. Comparison table with horizontal scroll.
responsive.desktop: Full dashboard grid with all sections visible. Charts and tables at comfortable sizes.

layout_hints.financial_story: The pattern should read from summary health to income/expense explanation to property comparison. Preserve a clear story of profitability rather than a loose collection of finance widgets.
layout_hints.breakdown_role: Income and expense panels should feel like explanatory companions to NOI and cash flow, not independent dashboards fighting for attention.
layout_hints.comparison_discipline: Property-level comparisons belong after portfolio context is established so users can move from aggregate health into individual asset performance.

motion.micro: Metric cards and chart controls may respond crisply on hover and focus, with restrained value or chart reveal behavior.
motion.transitions: Date-range or filter changes should preserve a sense of continuity in the financial story rather than making values feel unstable.

keyboard: Filters, summary cards, breakdown charts, and comparison surfaces should remain keyboard reachable in reading order.
announcements: Portfolio-level revenue, expenses, NOI, and comparison context should be exposed semantically so the dashboard remains interpretable beyond chart visuals.

composition.PnlDashboard: Container(d-section, flex-col, gap-6) > [Header + SummaryCards + Breakdowns + PropertyComparison + Projections?]
